title: Numbers: Wilderness Lessons
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of numbers: wilderness lessons and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study numbers: wilderness lessons and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What did Moses do at Meribah that disqualified him from entering the promised land? Answer: God told Moses to speak to the rock, but Moses struck it twice in anger instead.
segments.correct_answer: Moses struck the rock twice instead of speaking to it as God commanded
acceptable_variants: he hit the rock instead of speaking to it
moses struck the rock in anger disobeying god's command
he dishonored god before the people by striking rather than speaking to the rock

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The place of strife where Moses struck the rock in disobedience; the event that cost Moses the right to enter Canaan and illustrates that even God's servants face consequences for dishonoring him
The bronze image Moses made and raised on a pole in Numbers 21 so that those bitten by serpents could look at it and live; Jesus references this in John 3:14 as a type of his crucifixion
